region_focus: India — Andhra Pradesh (Godavari Belt, Krishna, Nellore, Srikakulam)
description: Brand → Product → Dosage → Use-case recommendation engine database

recommendation_engine_schema.how_to_use: Match: problem_keyword → category → filter by species + season + salinity → return brand + product + dosage + expected_result

medicines.compliance_warning: ⚠️ Antibiotics require veterinary prescription. Observe withdrawal periods. Non-compliant residues risk export market bans.

products.notes: First-choice antibiotic for most bacterial infections; residue-safe for exports
Broad spectrum; some resistance developing in Vibrio; culture + sensitivity test recommended

products.caution: Avoid probiotics for 48h after; toxic to fish at high concentrations — dose carefully

hatcheries_seed.critical_note: Seed quality determines 50% of crop success — SPF certification and PCR testing are non-negotiable

sandhya_aqua_hatcheries.notes: One of few AP hatcheries producing quality P. monodon PLs

sapthagiri_hatcheries.ap_reputation: MODERATE — serving small to medium farmers in AP

recommendation_mappings.description: Problem → Category → Best Brand(s) → Product → Dosage → Result
recommendation_mappings.note: This is the CORE of the recommendation engine

primary_recommendations.expected_result: Vibrio reduction in gut; FCR improvement

mappings.secondary: Reduce feeding 30%; check water quality (DO, ammonia)

immediate_actions: Apply Growel Pond Pro: 2 kg/acre to control Vibrio

primary_recommendations.note: Stop feed first, resume at 50% after stabilization

antibiotic_if_severe.note: Requires vet prescription; only if bacterial confirmed

primary_recommendations.dosage: 5 kg/acre in water + 10 g/kg in feed

mappings.ap_context: Critical in low-salinity zones (Krishna, West Godavari); apply after every molting and post-rain
mappings.water_action: Check salinity (should be >5 ppt); increase if possible; check alkalinity (target 100–150 mg/L)

immediate_actions: Do NOT drain pond to natural water body
mappings.decision: If crop age >60 days and >40% ABW target reached → EMERGENCY HARVEST immediately
biosecurity_lockdown: Chlorinate any water release: 30 ppm × 24h contact
Lime entire pond after drain: 1000 kg/ha CaO

supportive_care_if_early_detection.note: Immune support only; does not kill virus

supportive_care_if_early_detection.dosage: 2 kg per MT feed — immune modulation

immediate_actions: 20–30% water exchange if clean source available
mappings.ap_context: Very common post-rain and during summer; AP high-density ponds prone to this

immediate_actions: Run ALL aerators and paddlewheels at maximum IMMEDIATELY
Open water exchange if clean source available
Apply Hydrogen Peroxide 30%: 500 mL/acre in emergency zones

follow_up.product: Growel Pond Pro or Biostadt Terragard-SP
follow_up.note: Reduce organic load that caused DO crash

primary_recommendations.dosage: 150 mL/acre diluted (if Vibrio count confirmed high)

antibiotic_if_severe.requires: Vet prescription + bacterial culture sensitivity test

recommendations: PCR test immediately to confirm EHP
If confirmed EHP: evaluate early partial harvest of larger animals
Increase Vitamin C in feed: 2–3 g/kg
Use Kemin Clostat + Growel Gut Pro for gut health
Plan thorough pond drying and CaO liming for next crop
Source PCR-negative PLs for next cycle from Avanti or CP hatcheries

primary_recommendations.dosage: 10–15 mg/kg BW × 10 days in medicated feed
primary_recommendations.expected_result: Mortality stops within 3–5 days; recovery in 7–10 days

supportive.dosage: 2 g/kg feed — immune support during treatment

primary_recommendations.dosage: 10–20 ppm bath × 30 min — external lesion treatment
25–50 ppm bath × 20 min for severe skin lesions
10–20 ppm bath × 30 min; repeat every 48h × 3 cycles

mappings.temperature_management: Raise temperature to >28°C if possible to accelerate life cycle → faster kill of theronts

diagnosis_checklist: Check water quality DO, ammonia, pH
Check ABW trend — disease suppressing growth?
Check pellet size match to shrimp size

product_solutions.dosage: 2–3 kg/MT feed — if feed quality/storage suspect

ap_seasonal_calendar.description: Andhra Pradesh aquaculture seasonal product use guide

priority_products: Vitamin C in feed (2 g/kg) — heat stress management
Agricultural lime: 50 kg/acre after every rain
Growel Aqua Mineral / Intas AquaMins: after rain
Growel Toxin Binder in feed (feed storage quality risk)
Monitor water color daily — algae bloom instability
Increase feed to FCR target (good growth season)
Vitamin C + Vitamin E — cold stress
Consider harvest if reaching target weight before deep winter
